scope: final observability-contract closeout and claim ledger; no new data / retraining / rescue; not SOTA
final_verdict: Observability contracts expose why unlabeled offline-TTA cannot be safely controlled under honest prior uncertainty.

evidence_ledger.question: Can source-only (R0) diagnostics predict TTA harm?
evidence_ledger.finding: source-only harm-predictor at/below the majority baseline (TOS-1 ceiling).

evidence_ledger.question: Do richer R1 target-unlabeled diagnostics predict harm?
evidence_ledger.finding: R1 predictor within its permutation null at n_perm=1000 -> overfitting artifact, not signal.

evidence_ledger.question: Are the real minimal-label curves accurate or merely low-coverage?
evidence_ledger.finding: high precision when decisive but coverage-limited; k=0 non-identifiable.

evidence_ledger.question: Can R2 minimal labels safely control harm (static policies)?
evidence_ledger.finding: no deployable minimal-label policy adapts while keeping harm<=0.05.

evidence_ledger.question: Does sequential acquisition rescue it, and is benefit real?
evidence_ledger.finding: best sequential policy = none; benefit is rare, small, seed-unstable.

evidence_ledger.question: Is the failure an accuracy-vs-bAcc estimand mismatch?
evidence_ledger.finding: class-balanced grid -> accuracy==bAcc per run (Step-16 gap was a threshold artifact); estimand-invariant.

evidence_ledger.question: Is TTA harm global or class/prior-dependent?
evidence_ledger.finding: class-specific and prior-dependent; only a small minority harmful under all priors.

evidence_ledger.question: Is any run robustly beneficial under bounded prior uncertainty?
evidence_ledger.finding: with a harm margin tau>=0.05 no run is robustly beneficial at any rho; best prior-robust policy = none.

deployment_decision_ladder.rationale: TOS-1: target gain non-identifiable source-only; source-only harm-predictor is NULL (Step 12/14).
target gain needs labels (TU-2); R1 diagnostics do not predict harm above their permutation null (Step 13/14).
prior-weighted gain is a counterfactual under a DECLARED prior and ORACLE class deltas; the sign is prior-dependent (Step 18); not deployable.
robust benefit is unattainable under any honest uncertainty (tau>=0.05) (Step 19); identity/block is robustly justified for the persistent-harm minority.
minimal-label static and sequential policies fail to safely select adaptation (Step 15/16); safe selection needs (near-)full labels.
the oracle full-target gain licenses safe adaptation but is an evaluation-only upper bound, never deployable.

forbidden_claims.claim: unlabeled offline-TTA is safe to deploy
forbidden_claims.why: Step 15/16 (minimal-label policies fail) and Step 19 (no robust benefit under prior uncertainty) refute it.

forbidden_claims.claim: the target prior is identified from R0/R1
forbidden_claims.why: TU-1 boundary: identification needs C1 AND C2 AND C3; C14/C15 only DECLARE a prior / prior set.

forbidden_claims.claim: prior-robust adaptation benefit exists under honest prior uncertainty
forbidden_claims.why: Step 19: with a harm margin tau>=0.05 no run is robustly beneficial at any rho.

registry_forbidden_claims: target concept shift detected from unlabeled target
GLS gives the target prior source-only
R0 source metric reported as a target risk/gain/concept guarantee
R1 unlabeled-target balanced accuracy reported as identifiable target metric
declared deployment prior identifies the actual target prior
prior-weighted gain reported as holding under all target priors
gain reported as prior-robust without a declared prior-uncertainty set
declared prior-uncertainty set identifies the actual target prior
unlabeled offline-TTA is safe to deploy
the target prior is identified from R0/R1
prior-robust adaptation benefit exists under honest prior uncertainty
manuscript_ready_summary: Under R0/R1 the sign of the offline-TTA target gain is not deployably identifiable; the source-only harm predictor is null (TOS-1 ceiling) and richer R1 diagnostics are an overfitting artifact once permutation-controlled.

R2 minimal-label control fails: neither static nor sequential label-acquisition policies safely select adaptation; safe selection requires (near-)full target labels (oracle, non-deployable). The failure is estimand-invariant (accuracy == balanced accuracy on this class-balanced grid; the Step-16 gap was a benefit-threshold artifact).
Mechanistically the harm is class-specific and prior-dependent, not global; the gain sign is fragile (median L1 flip-radius ~0.165 from uniform).

Under any honest declared prior uncertainty (contract C15), robust adaptation benefit is UNATTAINABLE with a usable harm margin (tau>=0.05); the only positive is a zero-margin sign-level artifact on a vanishing minority. The robustly justified actions are abstain or block, never a deployable adapt.
C14 (declared point prior) and C15 (declared uncertainty set) support counterfactual prior analysis only; neither identifies the actual target prior (Prior-Decoupled boundary).
claim_boundary: Terminal closeout: no target functional is claimed identifiable under R0/R1; no deployable adaptation is licensed at any honest observability level; C14/C15 are declared/counterfactual, never identified target priors; the oracle gain is evaluation-only. No SOTA.
